published_prior_art.cite: BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 Theorem 4 gives a multivalued M-distinct vertex family, not the displayed omitted (2,3) M-class.
BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 gives a complete signature calculus only for the binary-instrument case; no multivalued exact-signature recognizer was found.

AvisFukuda1996 supplies generic reverse search, while BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 supplies binary-IV output-sensitive structure; neither gives the stated multivalued implicit-IV parent proof or delay guarantee.
BoushehrianBadriAkbariKiyavash2026 Theorem 4 proves the stated family is a set of M-distinct vertices, not that it exhausts multivalued vertices or identifies deficit regimes.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives a complete M-distinct vertex characterization for the binary-instrument case and, for multivalued instruments, constructs a set of M-distinct vertices and an exponential lower bound; it does not state multivalued exhaustiveness.

Theorem 1 gives necessary and sufficient categorical-IV counterfactual-distribution inequalities, and Section 4 describes convex-program computation of bounds and confidence regions for convex functionals including ATEs.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 92(439), 1997
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article establishes tight nonparametric ATE bounds under random assignment with imperfect compliance, providing the finite-IV LP background.
The article develops reverse search as a general enumeration framework, rather than an IV-specific multivalued signature oracle or parent proof.

The article establishes hardness of generic vertex generation for feasible unbounded inequality-presented polyhedra, so it does not rule out a specialized IV algorithm but motivates the need for IV-specific structure.
The article develops inference for intersection bounds defined by infima, suprema, and linear-program values, supporting the downstream finite-extrema inference motivation.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Exhaustively certify the full gauge-fixed (n,l)=(2,3) vertex catalogue, including its adjacency graph, to turn the one omitted vertex into an exact deficit count and a regression benchmark for the recognizer.
Prove the binary sanity reduction explicitly: specialize the gauge and canonical-basis language to l=2 and recover the published count 5·4^(n-1)-2^(n+2)+4 before attacking l>=3.

State and prove a restricted multivalued subclass on which the parent map is total and polynomial-delay, with explicit bit-complexity and signature-completeness certificates; this gives a publishable intermediate result if the full class remains open.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 with the field-tier multivalued signature-recognition and reverse-search program, retaining the certified (2,3) omission as its concrete regression instance.
